questions.question:
In which of the cases stated below is the following statement true?
"For every non-deterministic machine \( M_{1} \) there exists an equivalent deterministic machine \( M_{2} \) recognizing the same language".      



GATE CSE 1992 | Question: 02,xx
options: \( M_{1} \) is non-deterministic finite automaton.
\( M_{1} \) is non-deterministic PDA.
\( M_{1} \) is a non-deterministic Turing machine.
For no machines \( M_{1} \) and \( M_2 \),  the above statement true.
correct_answer: \( M_{1} \) is a non-deterministic Turing machine.

questions.question:
Which of the following conversions is not possible (algorithmically)?



GATE CSE 1994 | Question: 1.16
options: Regular grammar to context free grammar
Non-deterministic FSA to deterministic FSA
Non-deterministic PDA to deterministic PDA
Non-deterministic Turing machine to deterministic Turing machine

questions.question:
Regarding the power of recognition of languages, which of the following statements is false?



GATE CSE 1998 | Question: 1.11
options: The non-deterministic finite-state automata are equivalent to deterministic finite-state automata.
Non-deterministic Push-down automata are equivalent to deterministic Push-down automata.
Non-deterministic Turing machines are equivalent to deterministic Turing machines.
Multi-tape Turing machines are available are equivalent to Single-tape Turing machines.
questions.correct_answer: Non-deterministic Push-down automata are equivalent to deterministic Push-down automata.

questions.question:
Let \( N_f \) and \( N_p \) denote the classes of languages accepted by non-deterministic finite automata and non-deterministic push-down automata, respectively. Let \( D_f \) and \( D_p \) denote the classes of languages accepted by deterministic finite automata and deterministic push-down automata respectively. Which one of the following is TRUE?



GATE CSE 2005 | Question: 54
options: \( D_f \subset N_f \text{ and } D_p \subset N_p \)
\( D_f \subset N_f \text{ and } D_p = N_p \)
\( D_f = N_f \text{ and } D_p = N_p \)
\( D_f =N_f \text{ and } D_p \subset N_p \)

questions.question:
Which of the following pairs have DIFFERENT expressive power?



GATE CSE 2011 | Question: 8
options: Deterministic finite automata (DFA) and Non-deterministic finite automata (NFA)
Deterministic push down automata (DPDA) and Non-deterministic push down automata (NPDA)
Deterministic single tape Turing machine and Non-deterministic single tape Turing machine
Single tape Turing machine and multi-tape Turing machine
questions.correct_answer: Deterministic push down automata (DPDA) and Non-deterministic push down automata (NPDA)

questions.question:
Which one of the following statements is FALSE?



GATE IT 2004 | Question: 9
options: There exist context-free languages such that all the context-free grammars generating them are ambiguous
An unambiguous context-free grammar always has a unique parse tree for each string of the language generated by it
Both deterministic and non-deterministic pushdown automata always accept the same set of languages
A finite set of string from some alphabet is always a regular language
questions.correct_answer: Both deterministic and non-deterministic pushdown automata always accept the same set of languages
